title: branch-maintenance flow rename — Command Center flow graph
description: Proves the branch-maintenance follow-up flow is exposed under its renamed id in the Command Center flow-graph selector and executor-lane label. Fails on pre-rename code where the flow renders under its previous name.

pre_conditions.description: Command Center dev server is serving the flow-graph dev harness.
CDP-controlled Chrome is available on the recipe port.

open-flow-graph.intent: Open the flow-graph dev harness which renders the flow-type selector for every flow, independent of live run data.

capture-selector.label: Flow selector exposing the renamed branch-maintenance flow
capture-selector.intent: Capture the flow selector before assertions so reviewers see the renamed flow pill in the flow-type list.

assert-renamed-pill.intent: Verify the flow selector exposes the renamed branch-maintenance flow pill. On pre-rename code this pill reads under its previous id and this assertion times out.

assert-legacy-label-absent.intent: Verify the previous human-readable flow label is no longer rendered in the flow graph surface.

select-executor-lane.intent: Switch to the executor lane so the monitor node renders the human-readable flow label.

select-renamed-flow.intent: Select the renamed branch-maintenance flow to render its pipeline and human-readable label.

assert-renamed-label.intent: Verify the selected flow renders the renamed human-readable label. Fails on pre-rename code, which shows the previous label.

capture-graph.label: Renamed flow pipeline with its human-readable label
capture-graph.intent: Capture the renamed flow pipeline with its human-readable label for the PR evidence.
